steps.title: Look for financial motive
steps.content: The historians claim the policy was motivated mainly by perceived financial opportunities. The strongest support would show Henry assessing the value of church holdings before closing monasteries and confiscating their estates.

steps.title: Reject indirect evidence
steps.content: A reports monk demographics after the policy. B describes what some monks and local buyers kept or purchased, not Henry's motive. D describes economic motives for a revolt, not the king's reason for enacting the policy. The answer is C.
